channel.title: Dashboards on Paranormal Distributions
channel.description: Recent content in Dashboards on Paranormal Distributions

item.title: The Metric That Looked Fine Until It Wasn't
item.description:
The dashboard showed a 40% drop in sessions overnight.

You checked it once. You checked it again. You opened another tab and checked the same number from a different report. Still 40%. You sent a message to your team. Someone called it a P1. A meeting got scheduled for 9am.

The answer, when it came, was that the previous Sunday was a long weekend in three cities. Sessions always drop on long weekends. They had dropped the same amount the month before. Nobody had panicked then because nobody had been watching that closely.

item.title: Who Gets the Credit? On Attribution, Windows, and Dashboards That Lie
item.description:
There’s a meeting I’ve sat in more than once where two teams are looking at the same campaign and arriving at completely different numbers.

Team A says the campaign brought in 10,000 users. Team B says 4,000. Both are using data. Both are confident. Someone inevitably says “can we align on a single source of truth” and everyone nods and nothing changes.

This is an attribution problem. And it’s not a data quality problem, usually. It’s a definition problem wearing a data quality costume.
